title: micromeria teneriffae f. ramosa
intro: Micromeria teneriffae f. ramosa, also known as Canary Island thyme or ramosa micromeria, is a subspecies of the Micromeria teneriffae plant, a flowering perennial native to the Canary Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Micromeria teneriffae f. ramosa include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use through teas, infusions, and tinctures, where the leaves are typically harvested and dried to be used in herbal remedies.
